title: story-16-review-churn
description:
This is a continuation of story-16-review-churn. Story 16 remains in a non-converging dev/reviewer loop: the current window contains another successful reviewer run at attempt 9, and the flagged watcher note reports 9 cumulative reviewer cycles with the story still active in-window. There are no failed runs or retry clusters here; the concern is structural churn rather than an execution failure.

The pattern has persisted across prior L2 concerns and is not resolving. A healthy story should converge well before this cycle count; continued reviewer activity at 9 cycles indicates repeated rework without advancing the story to completion. The immediate downstream impact is ongoing cost and operator attention burn on story 16, with continued risk of more cycles if the pattern remains unchanged.

Evidence in this window does not point to runtime, provider, or queue-level instability. No failed runs, tick anomalies, queue updates, spend spikes, or prompt-placeholder leaks were detected in the flagged note. The most plausible area remains prompt-level non-convergence in the dev/reviewer interaction, but this document is describing the sustained churn pattern rather than assigning a root-cause fix.

evidence.summary_excerpt: Story 16 resumed reviewer activity in this window with another successful reviewer run, raising its cumulative review churn to 9 cycles and keeping it actively cycling.

escalation_reason: Story 16 review churn has persisted across multiple L2 intervals and remains active, now at 9 cumulative reviewer cycles with another successful reviewer run in this window. This is sustained non-convergence with ongoing cost burn, and L3 judgment is needed because the pattern has continued past the prior warn-stage concern without signs of resolution.
